name: Onfleet
shortDescription: Last-mile delivery management for FlowRunner. Create and dispatch delivery tasks, manage drivers (workers) and teams, define reusable destinations and recipients, and auto-dispatch routes — all backed by the [Onfleet API v2](https://docs.onfleet.com/). Authenticates with **HTTP Basic auth** using your Onfleet API key as the username and an empty password.

actions.name: Create Task
actions.description: Creates a delivery task, the core unit of work in Onfleet. A task has one destination and (optionally) one recipient. Provide the destination either by referencing an existing Destination ID, or inline via a structured address and/or [longitude, latitude] coordinates. Provide the recipient either by an existing Recipient ID or inline via name and phone. Optionally assign the task to a worker, team, or container, set pickup/dropoff time windows (completeAfter/completeBefore), add notes and quantity, or enable auto-assignment.

actions.name: Get Task
actions.description: Retrieves a single task by its Onfleet ID, including its state, destination, recipients, assignment, timing windows, and tracking URL.

actions.name: Get Task by Short ID
actions.description: Retrieves a task using its human-readable short ID (the 8-character code shown in tracking URLs and the dashboard) instead of the full Onfleet ID.

actions.name: List Tasks
actions.description: Lists tasks within a time range, optionally filtered by state. Results are paginated via a lastId cursor returned by the previous page. The From time is required by Onfleet; To defaults to the current time.

actions.name: Update Task
actions.description: Updates fields on an existing task, such as notes, timing windows, quantity, or recipient assignment. Only the provided fields are changed. Completed tasks cannot be updated.

actions.name: Complete Task
actions.description: Force-completes a task on behalf of the assigned worker (the task must be in the active state). Set Success to true for a successful delivery or false to mark it as failed/unable to complete, and optionally attach completion notes.

actions.name: Delete Task
actions.description: Permanently deletes a task. Only tasks that have not yet been completed can be deleted.

actions.name: List Workers
actions.description: Lists all workers (drivers) in the organization, including their name, phone, assigned teams, vehicle, and current on-duty status.

actions.name: Get Worker
actions.description: Retrieves a single worker by ID, including profile, teams, vehicle, on-duty status, and (if on a task) current location.

actions.name: Create Worker
actions.description: Creates a new worker (driver). Requires a name, a phone number in international format, and at least one team. Optionally provide a vehicle. The worker receives an SMS invite to the Onfleet driver app.

actions.name: Update Worker
actions.description: Updates an existing worker's name, team memberships, or vehicle. Only the provided fields are changed. The worker's phone number cannot be changed via this operation.

actions.name: Delete Worker
actions.description: Permanently removes a worker from the organization. Any tasks currently assigned to them become unassigned.

actions.name: Get Worker Schedule
actions.description: Retrieves a worker's schedule: the days they are scheduled to work along with shift start times, durations, and timezone.

actions.name: List Teams
actions.description: Lists all teams in the organization, including their name, member worker IDs, associated hub, and enabled self-assignment settings.

actions.name: Get Team
actions.description: Retrieves a single team by ID, including its member workers, associated hub, and managers.

actions.name: Create Team
actions.description: Creates a new team. Requires a name; optionally seed it with worker IDs and an associated hub.

actions.name: Get Team's Tasks
actions.description: Retrieves the tasks currently associated with a team's worklist (unassigned tasks in the team plus tasks assigned to the team's workers). Supports optional time-range filtering.

actions.name: Auto-Dispatch Team
actions.description: Triggers Onfleet's automatic dispatch for a team, assigning the team's unassigned tasks to its available on-duty workers and optimizing their routes. Returns a dispatch ID for tracking.

actions.name: Create Destination
actions.description: Creates a reusable destination that can be referenced by ID when creating tasks. Provide a structured address and/or explicit [longitude, latitude] coordinates. Reusing destinations avoids re-geocoding the same location.

actions.name: Get Destination
actions.description: Retrieves a destination by ID, including its resolved address and [longitude, latitude] coordinates.

actions.name: Create Recipient
actions.description: Creates a reusable recipient (the end customer receiving a delivery). Requires a name and a phone number in international format. Recipients can then be referenced by ID or looked up by name/phone when creating tasks.

actions.name: Get Recipient by Name
actions.description: Looks up a recipient by their exact full name (case-insensitive). Returns the matching recipient record.

actions.name: Get Recipient by Phone
actions.description: Looks up a recipient by their phone number. Returns the matching recipient record.

actions.name: Update Recipient
actions.description: Updates an existing recipient's name, phone, notes, or SMS notification preference. Only the provided fields are changed.

actions.name: List Hubs
actions.description: Lists the organization's hubs (physical dispatch locations such as warehouses or stores) with their name, address, and coordinates. Hub IDs can be used when creating teams.

actions.name: Get Organization Details
actions.description: Retrieves details about the authenticated organization (name, email, timezone, country, and enabled features). Useful as a connection check to confirm the API key is valid.

idealUseCases: Turn incoming e-commerce or CRM orders into Onfleet delivery tasks with the correct destination and recipient
Onboard drivers and organize them into teams, then auto-dispatch and optimize their routes
Maintain reusable destinations and recipients so repeat deliveries skip re-geocoding and re-typing customer details
Track task status, complete or delete tasks, and share tracking URLs with customers
Confirm connectivity and read organization settings before running delivery automations
agentIdeas: When a new **Shopify** order arrives (**Get List of Orders** / **Get Order**), call Onfleet **Create Task** with the customer's inline destination address and `[longitude, latitude]` coordinates to schedule a same-day delivery
After Onfleet **Auto-Dispatch Team** assigns routes, use **Twilio** "Send SMS" to text each customer their tracking URL returned by **Get Task**

When a **WooCommerce** order ships (**Get Order** / **List Orders**), create the delivery via Onfleet **Create Task** and log the resulting task ID and short ID to **Google Sheets** "Add Row" for reconciliation
